title: Samantha's Starlit Dream
author: Dady Fredy
subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Dady Fredy

pages.title: Samantha's Starlit Dream
pages.subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Dady Fredy

lines: The moon climbed high, the sky turned blue,
And Dady Fredy whispered, "This one's for you."
"Come close, Samantha, rest your head—
I have a story to tell you before bed."
"Once there was a girl so brave and bright,
Whose laughter could turn the dark into light.
She carried a sparkle wherever she'd go,
From the tips of her toes to the top of her bow."
"She sailed on a cloud past the silvery moon,
And hummed to the stars a soft, sleepy tune.
The dolphins below leapt high from the sea
And called out, 'Samantha! Come dance with me!'"
She twirled with the fireflies — one… two… three…
Then rode on a rainbow, happy and free.
The wind sang a lullaby, gentle and sweet,
And tucked tiny flowers around her feet.
She danced with the owls through the velvet night air,
With moondust like ribbons caught up in her hair.
The stars leaned down softly to whisper her name,
And even the Northern Lights shimmered like flame.
She drifted through clouds made of pillow-soft light,
Where dream birds were humming goodnight… goodnight…
The moon, like a lantern hung high in the sky,
Watched over Samantha as she floated by.
But even the bravest adventurers rest,
So she flew herself home to the place she loves best—
Where Daddy was waiting with arms open wide,
And whispered, 'I love you,' and held her with pride.
"So close your eyes now, Samantha, my dear…
Dady Fredy is always… right here.
Dream of the stars and the dolphins at play—
I'll love you tomorrow… and every… day."
Goodnight, Samantha.
Goodnight, anak.
